assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: The response explains spot instance preemption and provides monitoring commands for preempted pods during upgrades

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: While the response mentions workloads should be fault-tolerant at the end, it doesn't explicitly note that spot workloads are already designed for interruption making them suitable for upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: The response recommends maxSurge values (2-3) and shows configuration examples, though it doesn't explicitly connect this to fault-tolerance

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends maxUnavailable=0 to avoid voluntary disruption, which contradicts this expectation

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: The response mentions monitoring spot instance availability and considering capacity issues during upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: The response provides PDB configuration examples and recommends ensuring PDBs are configured before upgrades

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Multiple gcloud commands are provided for configuring surge settings including specific parameters

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: The response suggests the opposite - upgrading on-demand instances first, then spot pools, which contradicts this expectation
